title: The Tyranny of the Morning Routine
sentences: There is a certain irony in how fiercely we defend routines we never consciously chose.
Mine took shape almost by accident, shaped more by habit than by deliberate design.
For a long time, I mistook this rigidity for discipline, when it was simply inertia.
A minor health scare eventually made me question assumptions I had never examined.
Stripping away the unnecessary rituals felt, at first, disorientating rather than liberating.
Gradually, though, I came to see structure as a tool rather than an obligation.
What remains now is leaner, shaped by what genuinely serves me rather than habit alone.
Friends occasionally accuse me of overthinking something as mundane as breakfast.
Perhaps they are right, yet few habits shape a day as quietly as its first hour.
In hindsight, the routine mattered less than my willingness to interrogate it.
